content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement
(916) 322-5662          322-5660             322-5901          322-6441

July 17, 1985


Kirk Pessner
Nielsen, Hodgson, Parinello & Mueller
One Maritime Plaza, Suite 2500
San Francisco, CA 94111

Re: FPPC Advice No. A-85-156

Dear Kirk:

     This is to confirm our recent conversation regarding mass
mailing identification under Gov. Code Section 84305.

     Gov. Code Section 84305(a) sets out the information which must
be provided on a mass mailing to identify the sender. The last
sentence of Section 84305(a) indicates that a post office box may
be used in lieu of a street address if the organization's address
is a matter of public record with the Secretary of State.

     Gov. Code Section 84305(b) provides that if the sender is a
single candidate or committee, the name, street address and city of
the candidate or committee need only be shown on the outside of
each piece of mail.

     You asked whether the provision in subsection (a) regarding the
use of a post office box in lieu of the street address also applies
to subsection (b). I advised you that the provision in subsection
(a) for use of a post office box also applies to subsection (b).

     When Section 84305 was amended in 1984, this provision was
overlooked. We intend to include an amendment to this section in
our 1986 legislative proposals to clarify this point.

     Thank you for bringing it to our attention.

Sincerely,

[signature]

Jeanne Pritchard
Political Reform Consultant

JP:kt

sections.question: You asked whether the provision in subsection (a) regarding the use of a post office box in lieu of the street address also applies to subsection (b).
sections.conclusion: I advised you that the provision in subsection (a) for use of a post office box also applies to subsection (b). When Section 84305 was amended in 1984, this provision was overlooked. We intend to include an amendment to this Section in our 1986 legislative proposals to clarify this point.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether the provision in Government Code Section 84305(a) allowing use of a post office box instead of a street address for mass mailing identification also applies to the requirements in subsection (b) for single candidate or committee mailings.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC advised that the post office box provision in subsection (a) does apply to subsection (b), noting that this was an unintended oversight when the statute was amended in 1984, and the FPPC intends to clarify this through legislative amendment.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is an FPPC advice letter with clear question and conclusion sections. No formal 'QUESTION' or 'CONCLUSION' headers, but content clearly delineates Q/A. Letter format confirms FPPC response to legal inquiry regarding campaign finance disclosure requirements.

We intend to include an amendment to this Section in our 1986 legislative proposals to clarify this point.

embedding.summary: FPPC Advice No. A-85-156 addresses whether Government Code Section 84305(a)'s provision allowing post office boxes for mass mailing identification also applies to subsection (b) requirements for single candidates or committees. The FPPC confirms that the post office box provision applies to both subsections, acknowledging an oversight in the 1984 amendment and committing to legislative clarification.
